ORDER GRANTING SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT MICHAEL CUMPTON'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL CUMPTON'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH A TRADITIONAL BASIS FOR JURISDICTION (E.G. PERSONAL SERVICE) OR GENERAL OR SPECIFIC JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION-THE SOLE EVIDENCE PROFFERED BY PLAINTIFF-DOES NOT SHOW THAT DEFENDANT'S CONTACTS WERE SYSTEMATIC AND CONTINUOUS SUCH AS TO TAKE THE PLACE OF A PHYSICAL PRESENCE IN THE STATE. THEREFORE, THERE IS NO GENERAL JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILS TO SHOW SPECIFIC JURISDICTION. DEFENDANT DID NOT PURPOSEFULLY AND AFFIRMATIVELY REACH OUT TO CALIFORNIA TO TAKE ADVANTAGE OF CALIFORNIA BENEFITS. DEFENDANT CAN BE SUBJECT TO SPECIFIC JURISDICTION WHERE IT IS DEMONSTRATED THAT DEFENDANT PURPOSEFULLY DIRECTED HIS ACTIVITIES AT CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS OR PURPOSEFULLY DERIVED BENEFIT FROM CALIFORNIA ACTIVITIES OR PURPOSEFULLY AVAILED HIMSELF OF THE PRIVILEGE OF CONDUCTING ACTIVITIES IN CALIFORNIA AND THE CAUSE OF ACTION "ARISES OUT OF" DEFENDANT'S CONTACTS. THE CRUCIAL INQUIRY CONCERNS THE CHARACTER OF DEFENDANT'S ACTIVITY IN THE FORUM, WHETHER THE CAUSE OF ACTION ARISES OUT OF OR HAS A SUBSTANTIAL CONNECTION WITH THAT ACTIVITY, AND UPON THE BALANCING OF THE CONVENIENCE OF THE PARTIES AND THE INTERESTS OF THE STATE IN ASSUMING JURISDICTION. SEE VONS COMPANIES, INC. V. SEABEST FOODS, INC., (1996) 14 CAL.4TH 434. IN THIS CASE, PLAINTIFF REACHED OUT TO FLORIDA AND IT WOULD BE UNREASONABLE TO FIND JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/EHG

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN SCHIFINO'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH A TRADITIONAL BASIS FOR JURISDICTION, GENERAL JURISDICTION, OR SPECIFIC JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION AND THE EMAILS ATTACHED TO THE DECLARATION DO NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT DEFENDANT'S CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA WERE SYSTEMATIC AND CONTINUOUS. THUS, THERE IS NO GENERAL JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILS TO ESTABLISH SPECIFIC JURISDICTION. A DEFENDANT CAN BE SUBJECT TO SPECIFIC JURISDICTION WHERE IT IS DEMONSTRATED THAT DEFENDANT PURPOSEFULLY DIRECTED HIS ACTIVITIES AT CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS OR PURPOSEFULLY DERIVED BENEFIT FROM CALIFORNIA ACTIVITIES OR PURPOSEFULLY AVAILED HIMSELF OF THE PRIVILEGE OF CONDUCTING ACTIVITIES IN CALIFORNIA AND THE CAUSE OF ACTION "ARISES OUT OF" DEFENDANT'S CONTACTS. IN DECIDING WHETHER THERE IS SPECIFIC JURISDICTION, THE COURT EXAMINES THE CHARACTER OF DEFENDANT'S ACTIVITY IN THE FORUM, DETERMINES WHETHER THE CAUSE OF ACTION ARISES OUT OF OR HAS A SUBSTANTIAL CONNECTION WITH THAT ACTIVITY, AND CONDUCTS A BALANCING OF THE CONVENIENCE OF THE PARTIES AND THE INTERESTS OF THE STATE IN ASSUMING JURISDICTION. SEE VONS COMPANIES, INC. V. SEABEST FOODS, INC., (1996) 14 CAL.4TH 434. IN THIS CASE, DEFENDANT DID NOT REACH OUT TO CALIFORNIA. RATHER, PLAINTIFF REACHED OUT TO FLORIDA. GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW HOW ANY OF DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS ARISE OUT OF THE CALIFORNIA CLAIMS, IT WOULD BE UNREASONABLE TO FIND JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT GREGORY HARLEY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH A TRADITIONAL BASIS FOR JURISDICTION, GENERAL JURISDICTION, OR SPECIFIC JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION AND THE EMAIL AND LETTERS ATTACHED TO THE DECLARATION DO NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT DEFENDANT'S CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA WERE SYSTEMATIC AND CONTINUOUS. THUS, THERE IS NO GENERAL JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILS TO ESTABLISH SPECIFIC JURISDICTION. A DEFENDANT CAN BE SUBJECT TO SPECIFIC JURISDICTION WHERE IT IS DEMONSTRATED THAT DEFENDANT PURPOSEFULLY DIRECTED HIS ACTIVITIES AT CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS OR PURPOSEFULLY DERIVED BENEFIT FROM CALIFORNIA ACTIVITIES OR PURPOSEFULLY AVAILED HIMSELF OF THE PRIVILEGE OF CONDUCTING ACTIVITIES IN CALIFORNIA AND THE CAUSE OF ACTION "ARISES OUT OF" DEFENDANT'S CONTACTS. IN DECIDING WHETHER THERE IS SPECIFIC JURISDICTION, THE COURT EXAMINES THE CHARACTER OF DEFENDANT'S ACTIVITY IN THE FORUM, DETERMINES WHETHER THE CAUSE OF ACTION ARISES OUT OF OR HAS A SUBSTANTIAL CONNECTION WITH THAT ACTIVITY, AND CONDUCTS A BALANCING OF THE CONVENIENCE OF THE PARTIES AND THE INTERESTS OF THE STATE IN ASSUMING JURISDICTION. SEE VONS COMPANIES, INC. V. SEABEST FOODS, INC., (1996) 14 CAL.4TH 434. IN THIS CASE, DEFENDANT DID NOT REACH OUT TO CALIFORNIA. RATHER, PLAINTIFF REACHED OUT TO DEFENDANT AND SENT THE SUBPOENA REQUEST TO FLORIDA. GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW HOW ANY OF DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS ARISE OUT OF THE CALIFORNIA CLAIMS, IT WOULD BE UNREASONABLE TO FIND JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. THE STATE BAR DEFENDANTS SATISFIED THEIR INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. THE DECLARATIONS OF MR. TORRES-GIL AND THE OTHER STATE BAR DEFENDANTS UNEQUIVOCALLY STATE THAT THEY HAD NOTHING TO DO WITH AND HAVE NO KNOWLEDGE ABOUT THE OFFENSIVE VOICEMAILS. PLAINTIFF CRAIG MARTIN DOES NOT PRESENT ANY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE DISPUTE ON THIS ISSUE. MR. MARTIN'S DECLARATION DOES NOT SHOW THAT MR. TORRES-GIL MADE THE OFFENSIVE STATEMENTS. PARAGRAPH 4 OF THE DECLARATION INDICATES THAT MARTIN IS NOT SURE WHO MADE THE CALLS. NOR DO ANY OF THE STATEMENTS IN MR. MARTIN'S DECLARATION PURPORTING TO ATTRIBUTE THE CALLS TO MR. TORRES-GIL CREATE A TRIABLE DISPUTE. PARAGRAPHS 7, 8, 15, AND 16 OF THE DECLARATION STATE THAT MR. TORRES-GIL LEFT THE MESSAGES THROUGH THE STATE BAR'S COMMUNICATION PROVIDER ILD CORP. THE STATE BAR DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO THIS EVIDENCE ARE SUSTAINED. ORDER AND A SEPARATE JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Craig Martin telephone number 415-640-4803 in Propria Persona.
Danielle Lee of The State Bar of California telephone number 415-538-2517 for all Defendants.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Defendants Mark Torres-Gil, Lucy Armendariz, Sherrie McLetchie, Susan Chan, Donald Steedman, and Robert Henderson ("State Bar Defendants") motion for summary judgment by is granted. The State Bar Defendants satisfied their initial burden of production. The declarations of Mr. Torres-Gil and the other State Bar Defendants unequivocally state that they had nothing to do with and have no knowledge about the offensive voicemails. Plaintiff Craig Martin does not present any admissible evidence creating a triable dispute on this issue. Mr. Martin's declaration does not show that Mr. Torres-Gil made the offensive statements. Paragraph 4 of the declaration indicates that Martin is not sure who made the calls. Nor do any of the statements in Mr. Martin's declaration purporting to attribute the calls to Mr. Torres-Gil create a triable dispute. Paragraphs 7, 8, 15, and 16 of the declaration state that Mr. Torres-Gil left the messages through the State Bar's communication provider ILD Corp. The State Bar Defendants' objections to this evidence are sustained. Order and a separate judgment signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Hearing And Petition For Relief From Claim Requirement (Gov. Code Sec 946.6)
Off Calendar Jun-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Craig Martin telephone number 415-640-4803 in Propria Persona.
Danielle Lee of Office of General Counsel telephone number 415-538-2517 for Defendant State Bar of California.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion or argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CRAIG MARTIN'S Petition For Relief From Claim Requirement (Gov. Code Sec 946.6) is denied. The court lacks jurisdiction to provide that relief per Government Code 911.4(b). On 4/13/16, Mr. Martin presented his late claim application to the defendant State Bar. The State Bar properly denied it as untimely on 4/27/16. Section 911.4(b) provides that plaintiff can make a late claim application "within a reasonable time not to exceed one year after accrual of the cause of action'" In this case, Mr. Martin's causes of action accrued at the latest on 5/8/14. On that date, Mr. Martin filed an amended complaint in Martin II that accused Mr. Torres-Gil of leaving the offensive voicemails. (Par. 40, Amended Complaint in CGC-14-537725 (Martin II)). Mr. Martin was on inquiry notice at that time. "So long as a suspicion exists, it is clear that the plaintiff must go find the facts; she cannot wait for the facts to find her." (Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1111.) Mr. Martin's contention that his causes of action accrued at a later date when he received ILD Corp.'s case management statement lacks merit. Because Mr. Martin filed his application to file a late claim with the State Bar more than a year after the causes of action accrued, his petition is also untimely. (Gov't 946.6(c); Gov't 911.4(b).) "Filing a late-claim application within one year after the accrual of a cause of action is a jurisdictional prerequisite to a claim-relief petition. [citation] When the underlying application to file a late claim is filed more than one year after the accrual of the cause of action, the court is without jurisdiction to grant relief under Government Code section 946.6." (Munoz v. State of California, (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1767, 1779.) Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff CRAIG K. MARTIN, Tel No. 415-640-4803 appearing in pro per;
Atty. DANIELLE A. LEE, ESQ. of the State Bar of California Office of General Counsel, Tel No. 415-538-2517 appearing for Defendants Mark Torres-Gil, Lucy Armendariz, Sherrie Mcletchie, Robert Henderson, Donald Steedman, Susan Chan, and the State Bar of the State of California;
Atty. MICHAEL KIBBE, ESQ. of Godes & Preis LLP, Tel No. 949-468-0051 appearing telephonically for Defendants ILD Corp and Michael Cumpton.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS MARK TORRES-GIL, LUCY ARMENDARIZ, SHERRIE MCLETCHIE, ROBERT HENDERSON, DONALD STEEDMAN, SUSAN CHAN AND THE STATE BAR OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, the State Bar Defendants' demurer is a) sustained without leave to amend as to the first through tenth causes of action as to all State Bar defendants, b) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the twelfth and fourteenth causes of action alleged against the State Bar of California, c) overruled as to the twelfth cause of action alleged against the individual demurring defendants, d) taken off calendar as to the eleventh and thirteenth causes of action as to all demurring defendants. The first through fourth and sixth through ninth causes of action for violation of the Ralph and Bane Acts fail as a matter of law. Leaving a voicemail message with a racial epithet, no matter how ugly and laden with meaning the epithet may be, does not violate these statutes unless it constitutes or is accompanied by a threat of violence. (Civil Code 51.7(a) and 52.1(j)). Plaintiff has not alleged either a threat of violence against him, nor does it appear that he could do so. The fifth and tenth causes of action for violation of 42 USC 1983 fail as a matter of law. "It is well established that states and state officers sued in their official capacity are not considered 'persons' for purposes of section 1983 and are immune from liability under that statute by virtue of the Eleventh Amendment to the United States Constitution and the doctrine of sovereign immunity." (Bougere v. County of Los Angeles (2006) 141 Cal.App.4th 237, 242.) If it is plaintiff's position that defendants were not acting in an official capacity, then there is no state action to support the claim. Except for plaintiff's failure to allege compliance with the Government Claims Act as to the State Bar of California, the twelfth cause action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is adequately pled against all the State Bar defendants and is not barred for any of the grounds set forth in defendants' moving papers. Similarly, except for plaintiff's failure to allege compliance with the Government Claims Act, the fourteenth cause of action for negligent hiring alleged only against the State Bar of California is also adequately pled and not barred for any grounds set forth in defendants' moving papers. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege compliance with the Government Claims Act if he can do so in good faith. The eleventh and thirteenth cause of action were previously dismissed against all State Bar defendants as stated in the order filed December 9, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Dec-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Craig Martin telephone number 415-640-4803 Plaintiff in Propria Persona.
Michael Kibbe of Godes & Preis LLP telephone number 949-468-0051 appeared by telephone for Defendants John Schifino and Gregory Harley.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT JOHN SCHIFINO'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction is granted. The motion is granted because Plaintiff fails to establish a traditional basis for jurisdiction, general jurisdiction, or specific jurisdiction. Plaintiff's declaration and the emails attached to the declaration do not demonstrate that Defendant's contacts with California were systematic and continuous. Thus, there is no general jurisdiction. Plaintiff also fails to establish specific jurisdiction. A
defendant can be subject to specific jurisdiction where it is demonstrated that defendant purposefully directed his activities at California residents or purposefully derived benefit from California activities or purposefully availed himself of the privilege of conducting activities in California and the cause of action "arises out of" defendant's contacts. In deciding whether there is specific jurisdiction, the court examines the character of defendant's activity in the forum, determines whether the cause of action arises out of or has a substantial connection with that activity, and conducts a balancing of the convenience of the parties and the interests of the state in assuming jurisdiction. See Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc., (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434. In this case, Defendant did not reach out to California. Rather, Plaintiff reached out to Florida. Given that Plaintiff has failed to show how any of Defendant's actions arise out of the California claims, it would be unreasonable to find jurisdiction over defendant. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Mr. Martin indicated to the Court that he is no longer contesting the tentative ruling. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS MARK TORRES-GIL, LUCY ARMENDARIZ, SHERRIE MCLETCHIE, ROBERT HENDERSON, DONALD STEEDMAN, SUSAN CHAN and THE STATE BAR OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Special Motion To Strike The Complaint [Code Civ. Proc. 425.16] is granted as to cause of action 11, off calendar as to cause of action 13, and denied as to all other causes of action. As to causes of action 1-10 and 12, the offensive voice mails are too attenuated to be considered a communication in connection with an issue under consideration by a judicial body. Those voice mails did absolutely nothing to further the disciplinary proceedings. As to cause of action 11, the statement that plaintiff was suspended for failing to pass the professional responsibility examination was a statement made in connection with an issue under consideration by a judicial body. Accordingly, the anti-SLAPP statute applies and plaintiff must demonstrate a probability of prevailing on the merits. In opposition, the only evidence that plaintiff proffers is his declaration. That declaration mentions nothing about the 1993 suspension. The motion is moot as to cause of action 13 because defendant Eliopoulos previously filed an anti-SLAPP motion to that cause of action and the motion was granted. The parties agree that cause of action 13 is no longer part of the suit. (Plaintiff's opposition, 5:20-24; Defendant's Reply, 4:25-28 (fn. 1)). Order signed in open Court.

NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MICHAEL CUMPTON'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction - The motion is granted. Plaintiff fails to establish a traditional basis for jurisdiction (e.g. personal service) or general or specific jurisdiction. Plaintiff's declaration-the sole evidence proffered by plaintiff-does not show that defendant's contacts were systematic and continuous such as to take the place of a physical presence in the state. Therefore, there is no general jurisdiction. Plaintiff also fails to show specific jurisdiction. Defendant did not purposefully and affirmatively reach out to California to take advantage of California benefits. Defendant can be subject to specific jurisdiction where it is demonstrated that defendant purposefully directed his activities at California residents or purposefully derived benefit from California activities or purposefully availed himself of the privilege of conducting activities in California and the cause of action "arises out of" defendant's contacts. The crucial inquiry concerns the character of defendant's activity in the forum, whether the cause of action arises out of or has a substantial connection with that activity, and upon the balancing of the convenience of the parties and the interests of the state in assuming jurisdiction. See Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc., (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434. In this case, plaintiff reached out to Florida and it would be unreasonable to find jurisdiction over defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
